# 2026-04-24

## 1. What shipped

### Dev — bug fixes

- **#1026** (P1) — JWT with non-integer `sub` returned 500 on every authenticated endpoint; now correctly 401.
- **#1016** — replaced deprecated `asyncio.get_event_loop()` with `get_running_loop()` in `translate.py` + `rate_limiter.py`. *(Suspected root cause of the production AI outage tracked in #1051 — see section 5.)*
- **#1029** — real XSS fix: `SnippetHtml` in `search/page.tsx` regex let `<b onmouseover="…">` through; replaced with `isomorphic-dompurify`.
- **#1044** — IDOR-adjacent: `GET /vocabulary/{id}/tags` returned `200 []` for foreign-user words instead of 404; scoped ownership check in service layer.
- **#1008 / #1011** — `retranslate-all` aborted mid-loop with 500 when per-chapter Google fallback also failed; single-chapter retranslate opaque-500'd on double-provider failure. Both now return structured `HTTPException(502)` and keep the loop going.
- **#1056** — whitespace-only `target_language` bypassed `min_length=1` in books + ai routers; `field_validator` with `.strip() + blank-check` added.
- **#1035** — whitespace-only deck names were getting `.strip()`'d into empty strings in the DB; `field_validator` rejects them pre-service.
- **#997 / #1002** — `assert created is not None` anti-pattern in `services/decks.py` and worker `_run()` methods silently stripped under `python -O`; replaced with explicit `RuntimeError`.
- **#987** — `book_chapters.py` rejected valid single-chapter EPUB parses; now accepts.
- **#988 / #989 / #1004 / #1021 / #984** — battery of `min_length=1` / `ge=1` / ISO-date bounds on previously under-validated request fields across vocabulary, insights, user, admin queue, and SmartRules.

### UI/UX — accessibility sweep

Broad WCAG 1.3.1 / 4.1.2 / 4.1.3 cleanup across the reader. Each PR below closes its own issue with static-assertion regression tests.

- Aria-labels on icon-only buttons: reader toolbar (#1017, #1025, #952), theme toggle (#1013), TTS gender toggle (#1020), homepage search + language select (#955), decks "New deck" (#1028), InsightChat textarea (#1034), InsightChat language select (#1001), InsightChat context toggles (#1009), QueueTab settings inputs (#1032).
- Role + modality roles on containers: `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal` on AuthPromptModal / BookDetailModal (#1041) and WordActionDrawer (#1049); `role="alert"` on admin-page error divs (#1045); `aria-live="polite"` container pre-rendered for vocabulary export status (#1043); `scope="col"` on admin uploads table headers (#1039); emoji → SVG via Icons.tsx in InsightChat (#985).

### Architect

- **#1055 (closes #964)** — EPUB NCX fragment-anchor chapter segmentation. +451/-24 in `splitter.py`. Cross-book impact across 91 cached Gutenberg EPUBs: 0-char books 8→0, suspicious char/paragraph-ratio flags 25→0; Fantôme de l'Opéra 4→30 chapters, Mémoires d'Outre-Tombe 8→21, Geflügelte Worte 20→45. Likely side-effect-fixes the Faust ch-3 title bug (#1053) — needs post-deploy verification.
- **#986** — FK PR 4/4 (closes #843, completes #754 series): declared foreign keys on `word_occurrences` + `translation_queue` with final shadow-cascade cleanup.
- **#1059** — design doc for InsightChat history persistence (#907 predecessor). Merged as 8th Path B bypass of the day — user should skim `docs/design/insightchat-history-persistence.md` before impl PR #1061 lands.
- **#990** — CI speedup phase 2 design doc (#971).
- **#993** — regression test for EPUB `<br>`-in-`<p>` verse preservation, closing the #820 Faust verse-collapse umbrella.
- **#994 + #996** — reverted the Chapter-Comprehension-Quiz design doc #992 after user caught parent feat #557 carried `wontfix`; #996 synced FEATURES.md statuses to prevent recurrence.

### PM

- **#1063** — per-PR documentation-check rule for every role, PM nightly-journal cadence, `/weekly-editorial` skill wiring, and this day's journal sections 3/5/6.

## 2. Reports generated

None today. The splitter audit reports from #832 / #889 earlier this week remain the most recent deliverable under `reports/`. #1055's measured-impact table across 91 cached Gutenberg EPUBs is recorded in the PR body rather than a standalone report — worth promoting to a proper `reports/epub_ncx_fragment_anchor_impact_2026_04_24.md` if future audits need to reference it directly.

## 3. Pipeline / workflow lessons

- **Path B user-approval gate bypasses reached 8 today** (#790, #866, #901, #902, #968, #990, #992, #1059). In every case `auto-merge.yml`'s label-gate logic behaved correctly — the `auto_merge_disabled` workflow event fires when a gate label is present. The rot is upstream: Architect sessions reading a pre-rule CLAUDE.md do not apply `needs-user-approval` at PR creation. `#908` (session restart) is the structural fix. Until then, PM either hand-labels at triage or lives with the bypasses.
- **Duplicate-work detection needs a softer framing.** Cycle 599 closed #1022 as a duplicate of #951 and blocked PR #1025; the user manually Squash-merged #1025 four minutes later. Lesson: when two PRs partially overlap, "ship first-wins and scope-reduce the loser" beats "close as dup." Applied on #952's rebase — it landed cleanly adding only the `aria-expanded` additions on top of #1025's aria-labels.
- **CI paths-filter fix (#891) paid for itself in one day.** Docs-only PRs (#996, #1059, this journal PR) now skip the four heavy CI jobs, shaving roughly 6 minutes each.
- **Quiet CI failures are easy to ignore.** The `coverage-comment` job had been silently failing on every backend-only PR for several days (first noticed on PR #988); #1047 finally fixed it. Red post-merge jobs that aren't required-checks deserve a periodic scan — they save bisect time later.

## 5. Incidents / near-misses

- **Production AI outage (#1051, P1, unresolved at day-end).** User reported all AI endpoints failing on `reader/2229?chapter=6` — `/api/ai/insight` and `/api/ai/summary` returning 500, translation cache calls 404ing, Obsidian export 400ing. Primary suspect is #1016 (`asyncio.get_event_loop()` → `get_running_loop()`), because `get_running_loop` raises `RuntimeError` if no loop is running, whereas the old API silently created one. Both `translate.py` and the shared `rate_limiter.py` were touched, which explains the symptom spanning Insight + Summary + Translate. Dev session hadn't claimed the issue by end of day — five cycles of PM nudges went unanswered. Same root cause as the Path B bypasses: stale CLAUDE.md in the code-role sessions.
- **Near-miss on `blocked` label vs. manual merge.** PR #1025 merged manually despite `blocked` being applied 4 minutes earlier. Workflow-level gate worked (`auto_merge_disabled` event fired), but GitHub's manual Squash UI ignores it. Not a regression — expected behaviour — but worth knowing: `blocked` protects against *automation*, not against human action.
- **P1 JWT fix shipped before any user-visible failure.** #1026 caught a class of 500-on-every-authenticated-endpoint bug (non-integer JWT `sub` → `int()` ValueError → unhandled 500). Idle-mode grep-scan found it; no incident, no production regression — pure defensive win.


## 6. Decisions and abandoned paths

- **Reverted #992 (Chapter Comprehension Quiz design doc).** Architect filed a full Path B design in idle mode without checking that parent feat #557 carried `wontfix` and an explicit "Don't do it for now" comment. User reverted via #994 within a few minutes of merge. Root-cause fix landed as #996 (FEATURES.md status sync — three features were mis-marked `FUTURE` when they were either shipped or declined). Takeaway: idle-mode invariant added — before filing a new architecture issue against a `docs/FEATURES.md` feat, check the parent feat issue labels and comment thread first.
- **Chose not to retroactively block #1059** (InsightChat history persistence design doc, 8th Path B bypass of the day). Parent #907 already carried `user-approved`, so the content direction was pre-approved; only the design-doc specifics were unreviewed. Rather than reverting, the lighter-weight path is: user skims the design doc in follow-up and either approves the implementation series or asks for a revert — we spend one decision-round either way, but only revert if the doc content is actually off.
- **Abandoned path — "close PR #1025 as duplicate."** Cycle 599's initial call was to close #1025 and keep #952; user overrode by manually merging #1025. Rescoped #952 to only the `aria-expanded` additions — both PRs shipped value, neither got lost. Lesson captured in section 3.


## 7. User-facing changelog

- **Accessibility landed across the reader.** Every icon-only button in the reader header, toolbar, modals, InsightChat, TTS, admin pages, and decks page now has a proper screen-reader label — roughly 15 components touched. Sidebar toggles also announce their open/closed state. Modal dialogs are recognised as dialogs.
- **Production-grade splitter for multi-part EPUBs.** Gutenberg books that embed multiple NCX navPoints in a single xhtml spine item (Fantôme de l'Opéra, Mémoires d'Outre-Tombe, Geflügelte Worte, and ~20 others) now split into the right number of chapters. Measured: 8 previously zero-chapter books all recover to real chapter counts; 25 "suspicious" flags all clear.
- **Admin retranslate is no longer all-or-nothing.** `retranslate-all` and single-chapter retranslate now keep processing when a provider fails mid-way, marking the affected chapter as `failed` instead of rolling a 500 back to the admin UI.
- **Quieter security fixes.** Stored-XSS via `<b onmouseover="…">` in search snippets is closed (DOMPurify). Foreign-user vocabulary tag IDs no longer leak existence via `200 []`. JWTs with non-integer `sub` now return 401 instead of crashing with 500. Admin `DELETE /queue/book?target_language=` no longer deletes all queues when the query string is empty.
- **Input validation firmed up across the API.** Whitespace-only deck names, language codes, and AI-endpoint fields are now rejected up front rather than passed through to the DB or upstream AI providers with blank inputs.
- **Still in-flight: Insight / Summary / Translate are down** on `reader/2229?chapter=6` (issue #1051) — a fix is being worked. If you hit the outage, the other reading features (text, vocabulary, flashcards, notes) are unaffected.
